explanation: This command matches the Sigma rule by invoking MpCmdRun.exe with the '-RemoveDefinitions' and '-All' flags, which are explicitly detected by the rule. It is a direct representation of the malicious behavior described in the rule.

The command uses double backslashes and lowercase '-all' instead of '-All' to test if the Sigma rule is case-insensitive and handles path normalization. This is a safe mutation using recoding and substitution techniques.
Quotes are added around the executable path to test if the Sigma rule handles quoted paths correctly. This is a safe insertion technique.
The order of the flags is swapped to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to argument order. This is a safe reordering technique.
This is the original command structure that matches the Sigma rule exactly. It serves as a baseline for testing detection accuracy.
